title: Coding
description: Here are some of the software projects I have developed over the years using technologies such as Kotlin, Java, SQLite, and Android UI design with XML. Feel free to explore some of the ideas and experiments behind my work.

coding_projects.download_description: You can visit our GitHub repository to explore the full source code of this application.

project1.title: GradeBook
project1.description: GradeBook is an Android app that helps students store grades, calculate averages and weighted scores, and track their academic progress through an intuitive Material Design interface.

body.management: GradeBook allows users to organize their academic periods in flexible formats, from semesters to monthly systems. You can create as many periods as needed and register assignments or evaluations inside each one, making it easy to keep track of your performance.
body.interface: The application features a modern and intuitive interface based on Material Design 3 and Material You. It supports dynamic themes and customizable color schemes that adapt to the device’s visual style.
body.statistics: The app also includes a statistics module that visualizes stored grades through charts and summaries, helping users quickly understand performance trends and overall academic progress.
body.technology: Internally, GradeBook uses a local SQLite database through the Room persistence library, allowing reliable offline storage and fast access to records. The application follows the MVVM architecture to keep the code modular, maintainable, and scalable.

project2.title: TeamPyros Mobile App
project2.description: An Android application designed to monitor and interact with the autonomous fire prevention system developed by TeamPyros.

body.monitoring: The TeamPyros Mobile App allows users to monitor in real time the data generated by the autonomous vehicle and its sensor network. Users can view information such as the remaining amount of water or extinguishing agents, battery status, environmental temperature, and critical alerts including fire detection or toxic and flammable gas leaks. The app also stores a history of detected events, allowing users to review the system’s activity over time.
body.emergency: To improve safety in critical situations, the app includes an automatic emergency alert system. When a serious threat is detected, a 60-second timer starts, after which the application automatically calls the configured emergency numbers.
body.technology: The application uses Firebase cloud services to synchronize and receive data from the autonomous system in real time. Its interface follows Material Design 3 and Material You guidelines, incorporating the Monet dynamic color system that adapts the app’s color palette to the device’s wallpaper for a modern and intuitive user experience.
